hypothesis: In La-, Ce-, and Th-based 3D hydrogen clathrate superhydrides at 150–300 GPa, introducing an ordered 12.5–25% vacancy pattern in one interstitial hydrogen sublattice (creating non-integer H/M ratios) will increase the electron–phonon coupling constant and yield superconducting transition temperatures at least 20% higher than those of the corresponding fully occupied integer-stoichiometry phases at the same pressure.
